summary: Zhuge Dan; rebels against Sima Zhao at Shouchun - the largest of the 'Three Rebellions of Huainan'. With his garrison of 200,000 men, secret ally Wu, and son Zhuge Jing as hostage in Wu, raises the eastern garrison. The chapter's main action is the building siege; Wu sends Wen Qin and Wen Yang back to reinforce. Sima Zhao's army surrounds Shouchun in turn.
Sima Zhao; takes the field personally against the Zhuge Dan rebellion; encircles Shouchun. The chapter's slow strangle.
Jiang Wei; mounts yet another Northern Expedition - defeats Deng Ai at Duan Gu; presses west. Sustained tactical wizardry, decreasing political support back home.
Deng Ai; defends the western front against Jiang Wei; loses Duan Gu but reorganises.
Xiahou Ba dies. Killed in Jiang Wei's Duan Gu campaign - falls into a Wei ambush at the western frontier; struck by arrows. The defector who briefed Jiang Wei on Wei's weaknesses for years; gone.
Wen Qin; returns to Wu's command; sent back to Shouchun to reinforce Zhuge Dan; quarrels with him later in the siege; killed by Zhuge Dan on suspicion.
Wen Yang; returns to Shouchun with his father; after Wen Qin is killed by Zhuge Dan, defects (with his brother Wen Hu) to Sima Zhao.
Wen Hu; Wen Qin's other son; defects to Sima Zhao with his brother Wen Yang.
Sun Jun dies. The Wu regent and assassin of Zhuge Ke; dies of illness early in this chapter. Power passes to his cousin Sun Chen.
Sun Chen; succeeds Sun Jun as the Wu regent. Sends Wen Qin's force to relieve Zhuge Dan. Will depose Sun Liang and install Sun Xiu in chapter 113.
Sun Liang; the boy emperor of Wu.
Teng Yin; senior Wu minister; named in passing.
Chen Tai; with Sima Zhao at Shouchun.
Jia Kui; named in passing - already dead.
Jia Chong; Jia Kui's son; rising in Sima Zhao's service.
Cao Mao; the figurehead Wei emperor.
Sima Yi; named in passing - long dead.
Fan Jian; in Liu Shan's court.
Wang Ji; with Sima Zhao at Shouchun.
Chen Qian; in Liu Shan's court.
Quan Duan; Wu officer in Zhuge Dan's relief.
Yu Quan; Wu officer; dies in the Shouchun siege.
Zhang Ni; with Jiang Wei's expedition.
Zhu Yi; Wu officer in Zhuge Dan's relief.
Zhuge Jing; Zhuge Dan's son; hostage in Wu; Zhuge Dan's gambit pivots on him.

Zhuge Dan dies. After months of siege at Shouchun, supplies exhausted; defections begin. Zhuge Dan murders Wen Qin in a council quarrel; the Wen sons (Wen Yang, Wen Hu) defect with the news; sentries surrender the gates. Zhuge Dan is run down in flight by Hu Fen; whole clan executed. The Third Huainan Rebellion ends.
Sima Zhao; concludes the Shouchun siege; consolidates his hold. Lavish reward to defectors. Returns to the capital paramount.
Wen Yang; with his brother, leads Sima Zhao's storm into Shouchun; the boy general of chapter 110 now a Sima retainer.
Wen Hu; with his brother in the siege's end.
Wen Qin; killed by Zhuge Dan in a council quarrel; the catalyst for the Wen brothers' defection and the fall of Shouchun.
Hu Fen; the Wei officer who runs down Zhuge Dan in flight; brings the head.
Jiang Wei; the western expedition continues. Wins more battles; the eastern coordination with Wu collapses. Eventually withdraws.
Deng Ai; on the Wei western front against Jiang Wei.
Jiang Shu; Wei officer at the western front.
Huang Hao; the eunuch favorite of Liu Shan. First major appearance - the corrosive court influence that will rot Shu from within. Begins his ascendancy over the boy emperor.
Qiao Zhou; in Liu Shan's court; counsels caution against more expeditions.
Cao Mao; the figurehead Wei emperor.
Sima Wang; Sima clan officer; on the western front.
Yu Quan; named in passing - dead.
Zhong Hui; rising in Sima Zhao's service.
Chen Qian; in Liu Shan's court.
Guo Si; named in passing in historical recap.
Zhang Liang; named in passing (Han-founding strategist as allusion).

Sun Chen; the chapter's villain. Increasingly tyrannical regent; learns of a court conspiracy against him led by Sun Liang; pre-empts it; deposes Sun Liang; installs Sun Liang's brother Sun Xiu as the new emperor of Wu.
Sun Liang; deposed by Sun Chen for plotting to remove him; sent to a private estate.
Sun Xiu; installed by Sun Chen as the new emperor of Wu - initially seeming a compliant figurehead. Will assassinate Sun Chen with the Ding Feng plot in chapter 114.
Jiang Wei; the chapter's Shu side - yet another Northern Expedition. Defeats Wang Han at Houhe; clashes with Deng Ai. Sustained tactical brilliance, no strategic gains.
Deng Ai; on the Wei front; engages Jiang Wei's Eight Trigrams formation - recognises it but is still drawn in until Sima Wang's intervention frees him.
Wang Han; Wei officer; defeated by Jiang Wei at Houhe.
Jiang Shu; on the Wei front.
Sima Wang; with the Wei force; clashes with Jiang Wei in the formation duel.
Sima Zhao; in the Wei capital.
Xiahou Ba; named in passing - dead.
Huang Hao; the eunuch's influence over Liu Shan grows.
Sun Quan; named in passing - long dead.
Sun Jun; named in passing - dead.
Sun Hao; named in passing as a Sun-clan figure; will later be the Wu emperor at the conquest.
Teng Yin; killed by Sun Chen in the purge.
Ding Feng; in the Wu court; will be Sun Xiu's instrument against Sun Chen.
Quan Ji; Wu officer; killed in Sun Chen's purges.
Xue Xu; in the Wu court.
Zhang Bu; in the Wu court; one of Sun Xiu's loyalists.
Zhuge Ke; named in passing - long dead.

Cao Mao dies. The chapter's iconic act of last-resort defiance. Driven beyond endurance by Sima Zhao's insolence ('Sima Zhao's heart - known to every passer-by'), gathers a few hundred palace retainers and rides out himself with a sword to attack the Sima compound. Cheng Ji and Jia Chong's men stop him; Cheng Ji spears him from his chariot in cold blood. The emperor murdered in his own palace courtyard. Sima Zhao publicly disclaims responsibility - executes Cheng Ji's three clans for the act; the deed stands.
Sima Zhao; behind the Cao Mao assassination; ostentatiously executes Cheng Ji's clans as cover. Installs Cao Huan as the next puppet emperor. The Sima yoke complete.
Cheng Ji; the assassin who runs Cao Mao through; executed with his three clans on Sima Zhao's order as scapegoat.
Jia Chong; gives Cheng Ji the order to strike; survives the cover-up. The Sima Zhao executioner-in-chief.
Jiao Bo; Cao Mao's bodyguard; falls trying to defend the emperor.
Jiang Wei; another Northern Expedition; defeats Deng Ai near the Tao River.
Jiang Shu; on the Wei front.
Wang Jing; killed for refusing to denounce Cao Mao; loyal to the dead emperor.
Sima Fu; in the Wei court.
Sima Yan; Sima Zhao's son; first major appearance - will be the founder of the Jin dynasty.
Sima Wang; in the Wei court.
Chen Tai; senior Wei officer; weeps inconsolably at Cao Mao's death; soon dies of grief.
Xun Yi; in the Wei court.
Xiahou Ba; named in passing - dead.

Jiang Wei; the chapter's centerpiece. After his Houhe campaign Huang Hao's eunuch faction at Chengdu drives him from the capital - Liu Shan summons him to court, then plots his execution; Xi Zheng warns him; Jiang Wei flees to Tazhong on the western frontier to plant the famous *tuntian* (military-farming) colonies. The career's irreversible turn: from offensive Northern Expeditions to defensive retreat; the Hanzhong-Sword-Pass cordon that will be Shu's last line.
Huang Hao; the chapter's domestic villain. Steers Liu Shan against Jiang Wei; consolidates court control. The eunuch ascendancy that breaks Shu.
Liu Shan; the boy emperor in old age; soft on Huang Hao; permits him to drive Jiang Wei from the capital. The collapse of Shu's central will.
Xi Zheng; warns Jiang Wei to flee Chengdu; the friendly courtier who saves Jiang Wei's life.
Qiao Zhou; in Liu Shan's court; advises capitulation; presages the surrender of Shu.
Deng Ai; on the Wei front.
Sima Zhao; in the Wei capital; prepares the conquest of Shu in earnest now that Jiang Wei is sidelined.
Zhong Hui; named as Sima Zhao's choice to plan the Shu invasion.
Dong Yun; named in passing - already dead.
Jiang Shu; Wei officer at the front.
Liao Hua; with Jiang Wei's force.
Fu Qian; with Jiang Wei's force.
Jia Chong; in the Wei court.
Sima Wang; in the Wei court.
Tang Zi; with the Wei force.
Xun Xu; in the Wei court.
Zhang Rang; named in passing in historical recap of court eunuch corruption.
Xiahou Ba; named in passing - dead.
Zhang Ni; with Jiang Wei's force.
Zhang Yi; with Jiang Wei's force.
Zhuge Liang; named in passing in the recap of Shu's lost golden age.
Pei Xiu; in the Wei court at Sima Zhao's side.
